speaker: RICHARD
text: I have been studying how I may compare

speaker: RICHARD
text: This prison where I live unto the world,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And for because the world is populous

speaker: RICHARD
text: And here is not a creature but myself,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I cannot do it. Yet I’ll hammer it out.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My brain I’ll prove the female to my soul,

speaker: RICHARD
text: My soul the father, and these two beget

speaker: RICHARD
text: A generation of still-breeding thoughts,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And these same thoughts people this little world,

speaker: RICHARD
text: In humors like the people of this world,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For no thought is contented. The better sort,

speaker: RICHARD
text: As thoughts of things divine, are intermixed

speaker: RICHARD
text: With scruples, and do set the word itself

speaker: RICHARD
text: Against the word, as thus: “Come, little ones,”

speaker: RICHARD
text: And then again,

speaker: RICHARD
text: “It is as hard to come as for a camel

speaker: RICHARD
text: To thread the postern of a small needle’s eye.”

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thoughts tending to ambition, they do plot

speaker: RICHARD
text: Unlikely wonders: how these vain weak nails

speaker: RICHARD
text: May tear a passage through the flinty ribs

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of this hard world, my ragged prison walls,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, for they cannot, die in their own pride.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thoughts tending to content flatter themselves

speaker: RICHARD
text: That they are not the first of fortune’s slaves,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor shall not be the last—like silly beggars

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who, sitting in the stocks, refuge their shame

speaker: RICHARD
text: That many have and others must sit there,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And in this thought they find a kind of ease,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Bearing their own misfortunes on the back

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of such as have before endured the like.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thus play I in one person many people,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And none contented. Sometimes am I king.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then treasons make me wish myself a beggar,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And so I am; then crushing penury

speaker: RICHARD
text: Persuades me I was better when a king.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then am I kinged again, and by and by

speaker: RICHARD
text: Think that I am unkinged by Bolingbroke,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And straight am nothing. But whate’er I be,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor I nor any man that but man is

speaker: RICHARD
text: With nothing shall be pleased till he be eased

speaker: RICHARD
text:
With being nothing.
                 
                
                  (
                  The
                   
                  music
                   
                  plays
                  .
                  )
                
               Music do I hear?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ha, ha, keep time! How sour sweet music is

speaker: RICHARD
text: When time is broke and no proportion kept.

speaker: RICHARD
text: So is it in the music of men’s lives.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And here have I the daintiness of ear

speaker: RICHARD
text: To check time broke in a disordered string;

speaker: RICHARD
text: But for the concord of my state and time

speaker: RICHARD
text: Had not an ear to hear my true time broke.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I wasted time, and now doth time waste me;

speaker: RICHARD
text: For now hath time made me his numb’ring clock.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My thoughts are minutes, and with sighs they jar

speaker: RICHARD
text: Their watches on unto mine eyes, the outward watch,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whereto my finger, like a dial’s point,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is pointing still in cleansing them from tears.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now, sir, the sound that tells what hour it is

speaker: RICHARD
text: Are clamorous groans which strike upon my heart,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which is the bell. So sighs and tears and groans

speaker: RICHARD
text: Show minutes, times, and hours. But my time

speaker: RICHARD
text: Runs posting on in Bolingbroke’s proud joy,

speaker: RICHARD
text: While I stand fooling here, his jack of the clock.

speaker: RICHARD
text: This music mads me. Let it sound no more,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For though it have holp madmen to their wits,

speaker: RICHARD
text: In me it seems it will make wise men mad.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Yet blessing on his heart that gives it me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For ’tis a sign of love, and love to Richard

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is a strange brooch in this all-hating world.

speaker: GROOM
text: Hail, royal prince!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thanks, noble peer.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The cheapest of us is ten groats too dear.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What art thou, and how comest thou hither,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where no man never comes but that sad dog

speaker: RICHARD
text: That brings me food to make misfortune live?

speaker: GROOM
text: I was a poor groom of thy stable, king,

speaker: GROOM
text: When thou wert king; who, traveling towards York,

speaker: GROOM
text: With much ado at length have gotten leave

speaker: GROOM
text: To look upon my sometime royal master’s face.

speaker: GROOM
text: O, how it earned my heart when I beheld

speaker: GROOM
text: In London streets, that coronation day,

speaker: GROOM
text: When Bolingbroke rode on roan Barbary,

speaker: GROOM
text: That horse that thou so often hast bestrid,

speaker: GROOM
text: That horse that I so carefully have dressed.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Rode he on Barbary? Tell me, gentle friend,

speaker: RICHARD
text: How went he under him?

speaker: GROOM
text: So proudly as if he disdained the ground.

speaker: RICHARD
text: So proud that Bolingbroke was on his back!

speaker: RICHARD
text: That jade hath eat bread from my royal hand;

speaker: RICHARD
text: This hand hath made him proud with clapping him.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Would he not stumble? Would he not fall down

speaker: RICHARD
text: (Since pride must have a fall) and break the neck

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of that proud man that did usurp his back?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Forgiveness, horse! Why do I rail on thee,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Since thou, created to be awed by man,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Wast born to bear? I was not made a horse,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And yet I bear a burden like an ass,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Spurred, galled, and tired by jauncing Bolingbroke.

speaker: KEEPER
text: Fellow, give place. Here is no longer stay.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If thou love me, ’tis time thou wert away.

speaker: GROOM
text: What my tongue dares not, that my heart shall say.

speaker: KEEPER
text: My lord, will ’t please you to fall to?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Taste of it first as thou art wont to do.

speaker: KEEPER
text: My lord, I dare not. Sir Pierce of Exton,

speaker: KEEPER
text: Who lately came from the King, commands the contrary.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The devil take Henry of Lancaster and thee!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Patience is stale, and I am weary of it.

speaker: KEEPER
text: Help, help, help!

speaker: RICHARD
text: How now, what means death in this rude assault?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Villain, thy own hand yields thy death’s instrument.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go thou and fill another room in hell.

speaker: RICHARD
text: That hand shall burn in never-quenching fire

speaker: RICHARD
text: That staggers thus my person. Exton, thy fierce hand

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hath with the King’s blood stained the King’s own land.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Mount, mount, my soul. Thy seat is up on high,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whilst my gross flesh sinks downward, here to die.

speaker: EXTON
text: As full of valor as of royal blood.

speaker: EXTON
text: Both have I spilled. O, would the deed were good!

speaker: EXTON
text: For now the devil that told me I did well

speaker: EXTON
text: Says that this deed is chronicled in hell.

speaker: EXTON
text: This dead king to the living king I’ll bear.

speaker: EXTON
text: Take hence the rest and give them burial here.
